target.name: Polar Semiconductor
target.founder: Independent spinout of Control Data (2024 reformation)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $700M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Polar Semiconductor is a Growth-stage semiconductor company and independent spinout of Control Data, reformed in 2024. The target operates within a competitive Chips & Semiconductors sector containing 11 peer comparables—3 public (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private entities including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched.

Of the three valuation methods tested, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses the company's $700M total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier characteristic of Growth-stage advancement, yielding $840M. Peer-Median analysis was inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-filter matches within the same-stage and valuation-tier cohorts; while 14 same-sector peers exist, none of the filtered subsets met the three-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be executed because the target's revenue is not on file—a common data constraint for private-company valuations. The public sector median P/S of 20.6x (across ALAB, NVDA, AMD) remains contextual but unusable without revenue data.

The triangulation analysis supports a median valuation of $840M, with a range of $714M to $966M. This range reflects modest sensitivity around the stage-progression multiplier. Key uncertainties include absence of peer-relative metrics, undisclosed revenue, and limited visibility into the company's capital efficiency relative to other Growth-stage semiconductor ventures. Additional financial disclosure would enable revenue-multiple and cohort-based cross-checks.
